findings.summary: Realtime event type encodes DELETE payloads as non-null `new` records
evidence: Processor methods repeatedly cast `e.new` to concrete records to satisfy this contract (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:174`, `src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:248`).

Delete handlers already need `(e.old || e.new)` fallbacks, showing runtime payload shape differs from the type (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:316-317`, `src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:417`).
findings.suggestion: Model `RawDatabaseEvent` as a discriminated union by `eventType` (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) with DELETE using `old` as the authoritative row and `new` as nullable/partial. Then remove `as unknown as` casts and rely on narrowed types.

findings.summary: Shot-generation DELETE processing reads `e.new` and can emit invalid typed IDs
evidence: All `shot_generations` events are routed to `processShotGenerationChanges`, including DELETE (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:150-151`).
That method always reads `const newRecord = e.new as ...` and then uses `newRecord.shot_id`/`newRecord.generation_id` (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:344-360`).
Unlike generation/variant delete handlers, there is no `e.old` fallback for DELETE rows (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:316-317`, `src/shared/realtime/RealtimeEventProcessor.ts:417`).
findings.suggestion: Handle DELETE explicitly in `processShotGenerationChanges`: read from `(e.old ?? e.new)`, validate required IDs, and skip/guard malformed payloads before building `ShotGenerationsChangedEvent`.

findings.summary: Variant conversion returns untyped records and is force-cast to `GenerationRow`
evidence: `variantToGenerationRow` returns `Record<string, unknown>` instead of a concrete row type (`src/shared/lib/mediaTypeHelpers.ts:67-79`).
It sets `id` from `getGenerationId`, whose contract is `string | null` (`src/shared/lib/mediaTypeHelpers.ts:22-30`, `src/shared/lib/mediaTypeHelpers.ts:81`).
Callers silence mismatch via `as unknown as GenerationRow` (`src/tools/edit-video/pages/EditVideoPage.tsx:120`, `src/tools/edit-images/pages/EditImagesPage.tsx:210`).
findings.suggestion: Return a strict type from `variantToGenerationRow` (e.g., `GenerationRow` or a dedicated lightbox row), enforce/derive non-null `id` inside the helper, and remove call-site double assertions.

findings.summary: `task_id` is nullable at runtime but force-cast to non-null string
evidence: Code comment states generated types mark `task_id` non-null while DB behavior is nullable (`src/shared/hooks/useShareGeneration.ts:316-317`).
Insert payload uses `task_id: (taskId || null) as unknown as string` (`src/shared/hooks/useShareGeneration.ts:322`).
Compile-time contract (`string`) therefore does not match possible runtime value (`null`).
findings.suggestion: Align insert typing with runtime (`task_id: string | null`) by regenerating Supabase types or introducing a local insert payload type, then remove the `unknown` double cast.

findings.summary: Task param helpers cast unknown JSON to `string`/`string[]` without narrowing
evidence: `deriveInputImages` returns `(Array.isArray(images) ? images : []).filter(Boolean) as string[]`, which does not verify element types (`src/shared/components/TasksPane/utils/task-utils.ts:22`).
Several paths spread arrays cast as `string[]` from unknown JSON (`src/shared/components/TasksPane/utils/task-utils.ts:30-44`).

Extractor functions cast nested unknown fields directly to `string` in OR chains (`src/shared/components/TasksPane/utils/task-utils.ts:81-84`, `src/shared/components/TasksPane/utils/task-utils.ts:96-100`, `src/shared/components/TasksPane/utils/task-utils.ts:112-114`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce runtime guards (`getString`, `getStringArray`) that validate `typeof value === 'string'` per element before returning typed values; otherwise return `null`/`undefined` safely.
